resources.string: The mirror shows perfection; movement would only mar it.
MIRROR SHOWS PERFECTION. MOVEMENT WOULD DAMAGE IT.

Crystal mirrors repeat the flawless face until the world has no room left. Ghosts sing softly of victories that may never have happened. A scratch on the armor matters more than the blade that made it. The perfect pose is held for one breath, then one hour, then forever. Flesh cools into monument. The admirer and the idol become the same still thing.
CRYSTAL MIRRORS REPEAT THE FLAWLESS FACE. GHOSTS SING PRAISE. A SCRATCH MATTERS MORE THAN A BLADE. THE POSE BECOMES FOREVER.
